title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gum infection
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gum infection are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, used to treat and prevent gum infections. They offer a natural and less invasive alternative to traditional treatments. Examples include echinacea, goldenseal, and myrrh, which have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and immunity-boosting properties. These tinctures can help reduce gum inflammation, fight infection-causing bacteria, and promote gum tissue healing. By improving gum health, they can alleviate pain, prevent tooth loss, and enhance overall oral hygiene. Users report improved quality of life, with less discomfort and dental appointments, and increased confidence in their smiles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with gum infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and tannins, can help to tighten and heal damaged gum tissue, while also reducing pain and swelling. Additionally, yarrow tincture can stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria that can contribute to gum infection. Regular use of yarrow tincture can promote overall oral health and prevent the development of gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, helps reduce gum swelling and redness in gum infections.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property, attributed to the presence of alkaloids and tannins, combats the harmful bacteria causing gum infections.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property, caused by the tannins, helps tighten and heal damaged gum tissues.
Analgesic: The analgesic property, due to the presence of lactones and alkaloids, alleviates pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.
Wound-healing: The wound-healing property, attributed to the flavonoids, promotes faster recovery of damaged gum tissues and prevents further infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for reducing gum inflammation.
Leaves: Contain antimicrobial compounds, helping to fight off the bacteria causing gum infections.
Stems: High in astringent properties, promoting tissue healing and reducing gum inflammation.
Roots: Packed with antioxidants, helping to protect gum tissues from damage caused by gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, using scissors or pruning shears to cut stems at the base of the plant.
Weigh out 20-30 grams of dried yarrow herb for every 100 milliliters of vodka or grain alcohol needed for tincture.
Combine the herbal material with vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing the solids gently to release any remaining liquid.
Bottle the resulting tincture and store it in a cool dark place, with a shelf life of up to 3 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria causing the infection. The herb contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer over the mucous membranes, providing soothing relief to irritated gums. Marshmallow tincture can also stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria that can lead to gum infection. Its healing properties can promote tissue regeneration, helping to repair damaged gums and prevent further infection. Overall, marshmallow tincture can provide a natural and effective way to treat gum infection and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce gum swelling and redness, important for gum infection as it soothes irritated tissue and promotes healing. Active constituents include flavonoids and mucilage.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property aids in fighting off bacteria causing gum infection, crucial for preventing further spread and reducing the risk of complications. Active constituents include tannins and phenolic acids.

Analgesic: Marshmallow tincture's analgesic effect helps alleviate pain, an important aspect of gum infection treatment, as it improves patient comfort and allows for continued oral hygiene. Active constituents include triterpene saponins.

Wound healing: The wound healing property of marshmallow tincture promotes tissue regeneration, essential for gum infection recovery as it assists in closing and healing the affected area. Active constituents include mucilage and flavonoids.

Immune support: Marshmallow tincture's immune support property enhances the body's natural defense mechanisms, critical for gum infection management as it strengthens the body's ability to combat and recover from the infection. Active constituents include polysaccharides and asparagine.
remedy_parts: Roots: The marshmallow root contains mucilage, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in gum infections.
Leaves: Marshmallow leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help treat gum infections.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal infected gums.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains mucilage and tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair in gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots at peak potency when plant is in full bloom.
Clean and dry the harvested roots with a soft brush and air circulation for several hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle to ensure uniform size.
Combine the chopped roots with 80% ethyl alcohol and allow to macerate for 4-6 weeks in a dark glass container.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick tincture helps with gum infection because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herbs used in kinnikinnick tincture, such as willow bark and blackberry root, have been traditionally used to treat gum diseases and promote oral health. The tincture can help to soothe irritated gums, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent the spread of infection. Its potent natural ingredients work together to support healthy gums and improve overall oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kinnikinnick tincture helps reduce gum inflammation, providing relief from pain and swelling, due to the presence of tannins and essential oils.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections, thanks to the presence of compounds such as berberine and gallic acid.
Astringent: Kinnikinnick tincture aids in tightening and healing gum tissues, reducing the risk of further infection, due to its high tannin content.
Analgesic: The tincture provides pain relief by numbing the affected area, which can be attributed to its alkaloid content, including lobeline.
Immune-boosting: Kinnikinnick tincture supports the immune system, helping the body fight off gum infections, as it contains various antioxidants and vitamins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-microbial properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and fight off infection.
Bark: Has astringent qualities that can help reduce gum swelling and soothe irritated gums.
Leaves: Contain tannins that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of infected gums.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory and anti-bacterial properties that can help alleviate gum infection symptoms.
Seeds: Contain essential oils that have anti-septic properties, which can help prevent the spread of gum infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves and stems for the best potency.
Combine one part of the herb with two parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it with ingredients date and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tincture helps with gum infection because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's active compounds, such as alkaloids and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of periodontal pathogens, which can cause gum infection and disease. By promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing inflammation, wild indigo tincture can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with gum infection, and support overall oral health. Its natural healing properties make it a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments, without the risk of harsh side effects.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Wild indigo tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, important for gum infection as it can reduce the number of bacteria causing the infection. The active constituents responsible for this property are alkaloids such as indigotine and indigoquinone.

Anti-inflammatory: Wild indigo tincture reduces inflammation in the gums, which is important for gum infection as it can alleviate pain and swelling. The isoflavonoids, such as genistein and daidzein, present in wild indigo are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.

Immune-stimulant: Wild indigo tincture stimulates the immune system, which is important for gum infection as it can help the body fight off the infection. The polysaccharides found in wild indigo are responsible for its immune-stimulant properties.

Analgesic: Wild indigo tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is important for gum infection as it can provide relief from the discomfort caused by the infection. The alkaloids present in wild indigo, such as indigotine and indigoquinone, contribute to its analgesic properties.

Astringent: Wild indigo tincture has astringent properties, which is important for gum infection as it can help tighten and heal damaged gum tissue. The tannins present in wild indigo are responsible for its astringent properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild indigo contain active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective for treating gum infections.
Flowers: The flowers of wild indigo contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria, making them useful for treating gum infections.
Leaves: The leaves of wild indigo are rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of gum tissue.
Bark: The bark of wild indigo contains active compounds that have antimicrobial and analgesic properties, making it effective for treating gum infections and reducing pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried baptisia tinctoria leaves and stems in mid-summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Cleanly chop 20% of the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 80% high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years, dosing as needed at 20-30 drops per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb contains flavonoids, carotenoids, and volatile oils that work together to reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and promote tissue regeneration. When used as a mouthwash or applied directly to the gums, pot marigold tincture can help soothe irritation, reduce swelling, and eliminate harmful bacteria that cause gum infection. Its astringent properties can also help tighten gum tissue, reducing the risk of further infection and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for gum infection as it helps alleviate redness, swelling, and pain in the affected area. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: The tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infection, thus helping to control the infection. The active constituents responsible for this property include the alkaloids, saponins, and tannins.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for gum infection to allow for tissue repair and recovery. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, carotenoids, and essential oils.

Analgesic: The tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is important for gum infection as it helps manage the discomfort and pain experienced by patients. The active constituents responsible for this property include the alkaloids and flavonoids.

Immune-stimulant: Pot marigold tincture stimulates the immune system, which is important for gum infection as it helps the body fight off the infection and promote recovery. This property is due to the presence of polysaccharides and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are used in medicinal tinctures for gum infection due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are often used in tinctures for gum infection, as they contain high levels of antioxidants and have been shown to have astringent properties.

Stems: While not as potent as the flowers or leaves, the stems of the pot marigold plant can still be used in tinctures for gum infection, as they contain compounds that may help to reduce inflammation.
Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are sometimes used in medicinal tinctures for gum infection due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in amounts of about one cup.
Combine harvested flowers with a neutral liquid such as vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:3.
Allow the mixture to steep for four to six weeks shaking occasionally to release medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Store tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label accurately noting ingredients and date prepared.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb's active compounds, including asiaticoside, madecassoside, and brahmoside, can reduce inflammation, fight harmful bacteria, and promote tissue regeneration. These benefits can help alleviate gum infection symptoms, such as pain, swelling, and redness, while also supporting the healing process and potentially preventing further infection. By using gotu kola tincture as a complementary treatment, individuals may experience improved gum health and faster recovery from gum infections.
remedy_properties: Wound healing promotion: Gotu kola tincture can help gum infection by promoting wound healing, thanks to its active constituents such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and centelloside that stimulate collagen synthesis and tissue regeneration.

Antibacterial activity: Gotu kola tincture has antibacterial properties that can help reduce the bacterial load in gum infections, due to the presence of triterpenes and flavonoids that inhibit the growth of periodontal pathogens.

Anti-inflammatory effect: Gotu kola tincture can alleviate gum inflammation by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, attributed to the active constituents like brahmoside, brahminoside, and madecassic acid.

Vasodilation properties: Gotu kola tincture can improve blood flow to the gums, facilitating the healing process and reducing inflammation, owing to its active constituents like hesperidin and asiatic acid that promote vasodilation.

Antioxidant activity: Gotu kola tincture has potent antioxidant properties that can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, attributed to its active constituents like madecassoside, asiaticoside, and centelloside that scavenge free radicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for gum infection due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Stems: Gotu kola stems contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, making them useful for gum infection.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of gotu kola are rich in pentacyclic triterpenes, which have been found to have antimicrobial properties, helpful in treating gum infections.
Flowers: Gotu kola flowers contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating gum infections.
Roots: Although not commonly used, the roots of gotu kola contain asiaticoside, which has been found to have antimicrobial properties, making them potentially useful for gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gotu kola leaves from a pesticide-free environment or purchase from a reputable source in sufficient quantity for your needs.
Rinse the gotu kola leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris and pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Chop the dried gotu kola leaves into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle to increase their surface area for extraction.

Combine 1 part chopped gotu kola with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar, secure the lid tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the mixture daily to extract the bioactive compounds.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discard the solids and use immediately or store it in an amber bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, using 20-30 drops as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with gum infection because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as echinacea, help reduce inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain and swelling. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties fight off harmful bacteria that cause gum infections, promoting healing and preventing further infection. The tincture can be used as a mouthwash or applied directly to the affected area for quick relief and improved oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture reduces inflammation in the gums, which is important for gum infection as it helps to alleviate pain, swelling, and redness. This property is attributed to the presence of chrysanthellin, luteolin, and other flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: The tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infection, thus helping to control the infection and prevent its spread. The antimicrobial activity is primarily due to the presence of phenolic acids, such as cichoric and chlorogenic acids.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture neutralizes free radicals, which helps to protect gum tissue from damage caused by inflammation and infection. The high antioxidant content is attributed to the presence of various flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Immune-boosting: The tincture supports the immune system, which is crucial for fighting off gum infections. This is primarily due to the presence of polysaccharides, such as inulin, which stimulate the immune system.

Wound-healing: Eastern purple coneflower tincture promotes the healing of gum tissue, which is important for gum infection as it helps to restore the health of the gums. This property is attributed to the presence of echinacoside, a triterpene glycoside, which has been shown to promote wound healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective for treating gum infections.
Leaves: Contain tannins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria causing gum infections.
Roots: Packed with antimicrobial and astringent compounds, promoting gum tissue healing and preventing infection spread.
Rhizomes: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, aiding in soothing irritated gums and boosting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in late summer and early fall for optimal potency.
Combine one part echinacea purpurea with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture mixture.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the herbs to steep and release their active compounds into the liquid.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids then discard the solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool place for up to two years using it as needed or sharing with friends and family.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved coneflower tincture helps with gum infection because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's active compounds, including chamazulene and matricin, have been shown to reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and promote tissue healing. By using a tincture, the active compounds are easily absorbed into the bloodstream, allowing them to target the source of the infection quickly. This herbal remedy can help alleviate gum pain, swelling, and redness, while also preventing the infection from spreading. Its natural properties make it a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments, providing relief without the risk of harsh side effects.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Narrow-leaved coneflower tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for gum infection as it helps alleviate pain, swelling, and redness in the gums. The active constituents responsible for this property are chamazulene, matricin, and bisabolol.

Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of microorganisms, which is crucial for gum infection as it helps eliminate the bacteria causing the infection. The main active constituents responsible for this property are the essential oil and polyphenols.

Analgesic: Narrow-leaved coneflower tincture alleviates pain, which is important for gum infection as it helps reduce discomfort and sensitivity in the affected area. The active constituents responsible for this property are alpha-pinene, chamazulene, and matricin.

Wound healing: It promotes tissue regeneration and wound healing, which is essential for gum infection as it helps repair damaged gum tissue and prevent further infection. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and volatile oils.

Immune system stimulation: Narrow-leaved coneflower tincture boosts the immune system, which is important for gum infection as it helps the body fight off the infection and prevent future infections. The active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides, phenolic acids, and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-microbial properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and fight off infection.
Flowers: Rich in anti-oxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, promoting gum tissue healing and reducing redness.
Seeds: Contain essential oils that have anti-bacterial and analgesic effects, helping to alleviate gum pain and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots, weighing approximately 10-15% of the total tincture volume.
Chop the echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent absorption.
Combine the chopped echinacea angustifolia roots with high-proof ethanol or vodka at a ratio of 1:5, filling a clean glass jar to the brim.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for a minimum of four weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate solvent extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tincture helps with gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the gums. The herb's active compounds, such as eleutherosides and saponins, boost the immune system and help the body fight off harmful bacteria that can cause gum infections. Siberian ginseng tincture can also improve blood circulation, which promotes healing and tissue regeneration in the gums. Its potent antioxidant properties can further protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress. Overall, Siberian ginseng tincture can help alleviate gum infection symptoms and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Siberian ginseng tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for gum infection as it helps to alleviate redness, swelling, and pain in the gums. This property is attributed to eleutherosides, triterpenoid saponins, and phenolic acids present in Siberian ginseng.

Immunomodulatory: The tincture can help regulate the immune system, which is crucial for gum infection as it supports the body's natural defense mechanisms in fighting off the infection. Eleutherosides and polysaccharides in Siberian ginseng contribute to this immunomodulatory effect.

Antimicrobial: Siberian ginseng tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, important for gum infection as it aids in preventing the spread and worsening of the infection. The antimicrobial effect is due to the presence of eleutherosides, saponins, and phenolic acids.

Analgesic: The tincture can help alleviate pain, which is important for gum infection as it provides relief from discomfort caused by the infection. The analgesic effect is attributed to the presence of eleutherosides, phenylpropanoids, and polysaccharides in Siberian ginseng.

Wound healing: Siberian ginseng tincture can promote wound healing, which is crucial for gum infection as it supports the repair of damaged gum tissue. This property is attributed to the presence of eleutherosides, polysaccharides, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to promote tissue regeneration and healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of eleutherosides, which have been shown to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making them effective for treating gum infections.
Rhizomes: Rich in saponins, which have antimicrobial properties that can help to kill the bacteria responsible for gum infections.
Seeds: Contain phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce the symptoms of gum infections.
Bark: Has tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eleutherococcus senticosus roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Clean the harvested roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft-bristled brush and cold running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine one part chopped root material with 1:2 ethanol-based menstruum in a clean glass jar and let it steep for four weeks at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herb material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers tincture helps with gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain in the gums. The herb's active compounds, including tannins and flavonoids, can help to tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum disease. Cleavers tincture can also help to stimulate the flow of saliva, which can help to wash away bacteria and food particles that can contribute to gum infection. Additionally, cleavers have been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body to fight off infection and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cleavers tincture helps reduce gum inflammation by inhibiting the production of inflammatory compounds, thanks to its active constituents, including flavonoids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Cleavers tincture fights gum infection by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria, due to its active constituents, such as coumarins and essential oils.
Astringent: Cleavers tincture promotes gum tissue repair by tightening and strengthening the gum tissue, thanks to its tannins and other astringent compounds.
Diuretic: Cleavers tincture helps reduce gum infection-related inflammation by promoting the elimination of toxins and waste products from the body, thanks to its diuretic properties.

Lymphatic system support: Cleavers tincture boosts the immune system's response to gum infection by supporting lymphatic system function, thanks to its active constituents, such as coumarins and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the cleavers plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for gum infection due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: The stems of the cleavers plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for gum infection as they contain compounds that can help reduce swelling and fight bacteria.
Flowers: The flowers of the cleavers plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for gum infection because they are rich in antioxidants that can help promote healing.
Seeds: The seeds of the cleavers plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for gum infection as they contain oils that have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality galium aparine leaves and stems in mid-summer for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material using a dehydrator or shade drying method within 24 hours.
Chop dried galium aparine into small pieces and combine with 25% vodka or ethanol by weight.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container daily to ensure maximum extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum infection with herbal tinctures is by using a mixture of calendula, echinacea, and goldenseal. These herbs have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and immunostimulant properties that can help reduce infection and promote healing. Mix equal parts of each tincture, dilute a few drops in water, and rinse your mouth several times a day. Additionally, myrrh and tea tree oil tinctures can also be used for their potent antiseptic qualities. However, it's crucial to maintain good oral hygiene and consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal tinctures include periodontal disease and inflammation of the oral mucous membrane. Herbal tinctures such as echinacea and goldenseal are known for their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, making them effective in treating gum-related issues. Myrrh tincture is also commonly used for its antiseptic and astringent properties, helping to reduce pain, inflammation, and bleeding of the gums. It is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
